prompt:
Recommend the cheapest-CAPABLE orchestrator (engine / provider / model / effort)
for the NEXT session set after Set 094 closes. Output STRICT JSON only:
{"engine","provider","model","effort","reason"}. Do not self-opine beyond the
routed recommendation.

Context:
- Set 094 (Work Explorer module-first redesign, Set D) just closed. It was the
  091-094 release-boundary set: the extension is bumped to 0.42.0 and ready for
  the operator-gated Marketplace publish (tag vsix-v0.42.0).
- Per the operator-confirmed verdict, the NEXT set is **Set 095**: the "Hello
  World" walkthrough (re-homed 087 S4) that DOCUMENTS the new module-first UX,
  PLUS the comprehensive UAT re-cut for the redesign, PLUS the deferred
  getting-started.md.template teaching-doc rewrite and onboarding screenshot
  refresh. It is documentation + UAT-authoring heavy: a new end-to-end tutorial
  walkthrough, a rewritten scaffolded teaching doc (touches cold-start goldens +
  the dist template bundle), and a UAT checklist authored to a HIGH operator
  quality bar (literal copy-pasteable HumanAction steps, live-dogfood-first,
  per-walk preamble + order map; the 078 checklist is the exemplar). Little to
  no new production TypeScript; the risk is prose fidelity + walk followability,
  not architecture.
- Candidate engines/models (2026): claude-opus-4-8 (most capable, priciest);
  claude-sonnet-5 / claude-fable-5 (mid); gpt-5-x; gemini-3-pro. Effort:
  low/medium/high.
- Constraint: verification of Set 095 must run cross-provider (a different
  provider than the orchestrator's), so don't pick an orchestrator that starves
  the verifier pool.

Weigh capability against cost for a documentation/UAT-authoring set with a high
prose-quality bar and negligible architectural novelty.
content:
```json
{
  "engine": "claude",
  "provider": "Anthropic",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-5",
  "effort": "medium",
  "reason": "The set is documentation-heavy with a high prose quality bar but lacks architectural novelty. claude-sonnet-5 provides the best cost-to-capability ratio for this workload. It is capable of generating the required high-fidelity prose and structured UAT checklists, with the trade-off being a medium operator effort for prompt engineering and verification to meet the high quality standard. This is more economical than employing a top-tier model at a significantly higher cost for a non-research task, and it leaves gpt-5-x and gemini-3-pro as distinct options for cross-provider verification."
}
```
